posts.author: Business Standard
posts.text: Indian pharma stocks fall as Trump warns of 200% tariff on generic drugs

posts.author: pavinjoseph
posts.text: Trump proposes steep tariffs on imported generic drugs — Most tariffs were repealed by US courts as illegal, what’s to say this won’t be too? Even if the courts don’t intervene, one need only wait for the 100% tariffs to hit for generics (which account for 90% of US prescriptions) and the inevitable backlash that follows. 🫯

posts.author: BFSI News
posts.text: Indian shares fall on higher crude; pharma slips on Trump tariff plan

posts.author: Rohith_Raj
posts.text: Trump proposes steep tariffs on imported generic drugs — The U.S. relies heavily on imported generic medicines, with India being one of its largest suppliers. The proposed policy is designed to encourage pharmaceutical companies to manufacture these medicines within the United States instead of importing them. image 644×890 79.5 KB Here’s how it works: August 1, 2026 onwards: Generic drug imports into the U.S. would continue to enjoy 0% tariffs for two years , giving companies time to set up manufacturing in the U.S. After the two-year window: Companies that continue importing instead of produ…
